commands.review_summary:
The PR reuses the existing tool-use/result pairing repair for context-engine assembled transcripts in both initial attempt assembly and ownsCompaction loop views, with focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +67. Total +86 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main directly uses context-engine assembled messages while the OpenAI-compatible adapter serializes toolResult as role tool. I did not run artifact-producing tests in this read-only review, but the failing shape is clear and covered by the PR tests.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; this PR is ready for normal draft, CI, and maintainer review handling.

target.title: fix(agents): repair context-engine tool-result pairing
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88561.

Context-engine assembly could replace the attempt transcript after the existing truncation-time tool-use/result repair had already run. If the assembled messages contained a free-floating `toolResult`, the OpenAI chat-completions adapter later serialized it as a `role: "tool"` message without a preceding assistant `tool_calls` entry, which strict OpenAI-compatible providers reject.

This PR reuses the existing attempt-level pairing repair after context-engine `assemble()` returns, before assembled transcripts are used by the active session or the ownsCompaction loop-hook prompt path. It also keeps the pre-existing post-truncation repair on the same helper so all three repair sites use identical policy.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Context-engine assembled transcripts from both initial attempt assembly and ownsCompaction loop assembly no longer leave orphaned `toolResult` messages in the prompt path.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR worktree at `f7cce4167ab2` on Node `v26.0.0`, using production modules through `node --import tsx` rather than a Vitest/mock runner.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx ../openclaw-88561-real-proof.mts`
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the production-module probe:

```json
{
  "initialAssembleRoles": [
    "user"
  ],
  "initialAssembleHasOrphanToolResult": false,
  "loopAssembleRoles": [
    "user"
  ],
  "loopAssembleHasOrphanToolResult": false
}
```

Observed result after fix: The same repair removes the orphan tool result from both the initial assembled context and the ownsCompaction loop assembled view, leaving only the user context that can be serialized safely for the next model request.
What was not tested: Live WeChat/lossless-claw model-switch repro was not run; the live proof is a local production-module terminal probe plus focused regression coverage for the issue shape.

## Verification

- `node --import tsx ../openclaw-88561-real-proof.mts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --testNamePattern "repairs tool-result pairing after context engine assembly"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-context-guard.test.ts --testNamePattern "repairs tool-result pairing in ownsCompaction assembled loop views"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-context-guard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings after the initial patch and again after the loop-hook follow-up
